entry.title: Through situation handling need to send an Email notification to PR creator when PO is approved
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I am looking to configure Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA to automatically send an email notification to the original Purchase Requisition (PR) creator once the corresponding Purchase Order (PO) is fully approved.

Could someone please provide a detailed, step-by-step guide on how to achieve this scenario?

Specifically, I would like guidance on:

  1. Standard Situation Object / Template: Is there a standard SAP Situation Handling template available for this scenario, or do we need to create a custom Situation Scenario and Situation Display Type?

  2. Recipient Configuration: How do we dynamically route the notification/email specifically to the PR creator linked to the line item(s) in the approved PO?

  3. Notification & Email Template: How to link the appropriate Email Template (via Manage Situation Types / Maintain Email Templates) so the PR creator receives both an in-app notification and an email?

  4. App Configurations: Which Fiori apps need to be used (e.g., Manage Situation Scenarios, Manage Situation Types, Manage Teams and Responsibilities)?

entry.title: Re: Celebrate Your SAP Academic Community Conference experience: Earn Your Exclusive Conference Badg
entry.content:
Just a few days after celebrating my 30-year anniversary at SAP, I had the privilege of attending my first SAP Academic Community Conference North America at the University of California, Irvine – Paul Merage School of Business.

While I shared my reflections on LinkedIn, I also wanted to take a moment to thank this incredible community directly.

To the SAP University Alliances team, the faculty members, and my SAP colleagues—thank you for welcoming me into such an inspiring network.

As someone who has spent three decades at SAP while also serving as an Adjunct Faculty Member at West Chester University of Pennsylvania, this conference brought together two passions that have shaped my career: enterprise technology and higher education.

It was an honor to present alongside Isadora Royer and Ana Luiza Treichel Vianna on the SAP Campus Ambassador Program, showcasing how our SAP colleagues can partner with universities to mentor students, collaborate with faculty, and help prepare the next generation of business and technology leaders.

What I will remember most, however, is the people.

I met faculty members from across North America who are deeply committed to preparing students for an AI-enabled future. I experienced firsthand the passion and dedication of the SAP University Alliances team, whose educational resources and programs make a real difference in classrooms around the world. And I was welcomed into a community where many members have been collaborating, learning from one another, and building lasting friendships for more than 15 years.

The keynote sessions, customer and partner presentations, and conversations throughout the conference reinforced something I have always believed: the future of innovation depends on strong partnerships between academia and industry.

A special thank you to Evan Sevits and the entire SAP University Alliances North America team for the invitation, the opportunity to present, and for organizing such an outstanding event.

I leave this conference inspired, with new friendships, new ideas, and an even stronger commitment to supporting our academic community and helping students connect classroom learning with real-world impact.

Thank you for making a first-time attendee feel like part of the family.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 - Introduction...
entry.content:
Dear @rince_reji,

Question 1.

Yes. SAP states that the automatic conversion and assignment apply to all customers during the upgrade to release 2608. Public Cloud supports both 2-system and 3-system landscapes and SAP does not document any landscape-specific exception for this conversion. Therefore the conversion also applies to customers operating in a 2-System Landscape

Question 2.

For non-pilot customers, SAP does not document any mandatory manual reassignment or post-upgrade correction activity. The profiles are assigned automatically through Silent Data Migration. Existing depreciation keys remain unchanged and the depreciation calculation is not affected. After the upgrade the converted depreciation profiles are displayed in the Valuation section of the asset master record next to the depreciation keys. Non-pilot customers can see them but cannot use them. This is a visible UI change without functional impact, and it is worth informing your asset accounting users about it in advance

Pilot customers must additionally recalculate depreciation and review processes or validation rules that refer directly to depreciation keys

Question 3.

For depreciation keys that already exist at the time of the upgrade, SAP documents no exception. All existing depreciation keys are converted during the upgrade to 2608 and the previous depreciation keys are retained

There is one documented limitation and it concerns exactly the case you are asking about. On-the-fly conversion of changed or newly created depreciation keys is listed under a later release, not under 2608. SAP names expert configuration and the scoping of a new country as the examples. A depreciation key created or changed through expert configuration after the 2608 upgrade is therefore not covered by the documented on-the-fly conversion mechanism in release 2608

For non-pilot customers this has no practical effect, since depreciation continues to be calculated using the depreciation key. This becomes relevant mainly for pilot customers if a depreciation key is created or changed after the upgrade, for example through expert configuration or after scoping a new country

More details below:

SAP Help – Transition from Depreciation Keys to Depreciation Profiles

SAP Help System Landscapes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Summary:

For non-pilot 2SL customers the conversion and the assignment to asset master records are automatic. SAP does not document any mandatory manual activity and no exception is documented for SAP-delivered or expert-configured depreciation keys that already exist at the time of the upgrade. The only documented limitation concerns depreciation keys created or changed after the upgrade as described under question 3

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Executing Custom ABAP Programs in SAP BW Bridge Process Chains Using Custom Process Types
entry.content:
Executing Custom ABAP Programs in SAP BW Bridge Process Chains Using Custom Process Types

Introduction

One of the common questions while working with SAP Datasphere, BW Bridge is how to execute custom ABAP programs as part of a Process Chain. Developers familiar with SAP BW or SAP BW/4HANA are accustomed to executing ABAP programs directly from Process Chains (shown below) for validations, prerequisite checks, or custom processing.

SAP BW Bridge provides a supported extensibility framework through Custom Process Types, allowing developers to integrate customer-specific ABAP logic into Process Chains. By implementing a simple ABAP class and registering it as a Custom Process Type, the Process Chain framework can execute custom logic as part of its normal execution flow.

In this blog, we'll walk through the complete implementation using a simple business scenario. We'll develop the ABAP implementation, register it as a Custom Process Type, and execute it within a Process Chain.

Business Scenario

Let's assume you have two dependent Process Chains.

The first Process Chain loads and activates data into an ADSO. The second Process Chain performs downstream activities such as report generation or additional data processing.

Before starting the second Process Chain, we want to verify that today's ADSO activation completed successfully. If the validation succeeds, the Process Chain continues as expected. Otherwise, it stops and informs the administrator that the prerequisite load has not completed successfully.

We'll implement this validation by executing a custom ABAP program through a Custom Process Type.

Solution Overview:

The following diagram illustrates the overall solution.

When the Process Chain reaches the Custom Process Type, the BW Bridge framework invokes the ABAP implementation class. The class executes the custom validation logic and returns the execution status back to the Process Chain. Depending on the returned status, the Process Chain either continues with the next step or follows its configured error path.

Developing the ABAP Implementation:

Every Custom Process Type is backed by an ABAP implementation class. Create a new global ABAP class with the following properties.

Property

Value

Class

ZCL_CPT_ABAP1

Superclass

CL_RSPC_SIMPLE_PROCESS_TYPE

Interface

IF_RSPC_SIMPLE_PROCESS_TYPE

The implementation consists of two methods.

  • CLASS_CONSTRUCTOR – Registers the implementation class with the Custom Process Type framework.
  • EXECUTE – Contains the custom ABAP logic that is executed during the Process Chain run.

The CLASS_CONSTRUCTOR method associates the technical process type with the implementing ABAP class. Ensure the values assigned to c_type and c_class match the values maintained later while creating the Custom Process Type.

  METHOD class_constructor.

    c_type = 'ZCPT_ABAP1'.

    c_class = 'ZCL_CPT_ABAP1'.

  ENDMETHOD.

The EXECUTE method contains the business logic executed by the Process Chain.

For this example, the implementation validates whether the ADSO activation completed successfully on the current day by reading the Process Chain execution log (RSPCPROCESSLOG).

 "Get current system date

 DATA(lv_date) = cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( ).

 " Get details of the ADSO activation today

 SELECT *

   FROM rspcprocesslog

   WHERE type = 'ADSOACT'

   AND state = 'G'

   AND batchdate = @LV_date

   AND variante = 'ZAKADSO2ACT' " ADSO Activation variant name

   INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_reccount).

If the validation succeeds, the process returns a successful status. Otherwise, it returns a failure status together with an informative runtime message displayed in the Process Chain monitor.

The execution status is controlled using the E_FAILED parameter.

e_failed = ' '.     " Set process step status to GREEN

e_failed = 'X'.     " Set process step status to RED

Messages returned through ET_MESSAGE are displayed directly in the Process Chain monitor, making it easy for administrators to understand the execution result.

The complete implementation is shown below.

Creating the Custom Process Type

After activating the implementation class, open the BW Bridge Cockpit and navigate to Custom Process Types.

Choose Create and maintain the required details.

After saving, the Custom Process Type becomes available in the Process Chain editor.

Creating the Process Chain:

Create a new process chain, provide the required technical name and description.

Insert the newly created Process Variant. Connect it to the appropriate predecessor and successor steps. The Custom Process Type now behaves like any standard Process Chain step and participates in the normal execution flow.

Executing the Process Chain:

Failed Execution

If no successful ADSO activation is found, the validation fails and the Process Chain follows its configured error path.

The following message is displayed in the Process Chain log.

Data NOT loaded to ADSO ZAKADSO2 today. Please verify the prerequisite Process Chain.

Successful Execution

If the ADSO activation is found for the current day, the validation succeeds and the Process Chain continues with the subsequent steps. The following message is displayed in the Process Chain log.

Data successfully loaded to ADSO ZAKADSO2 today.

Conclusion

Custom Process Types provide a flexible and SAP-supported way to execute customer-specific ABAP programs within SAP BW Bridge Process Chains.

In this blog, we developed an ABAP implementation class, registered it as a Custom Process Type, and incorporated it into a Process Chain to validate a prerequisite ADSO activation. Although the example demonstrates a simple dummy validation scenario, the same implementation pattern can be adapted to execute a wide range of customer-specific ABAP programs within the BW Bridge Process Chain framework.

entry.title: Re: Power BI X S/4HANA Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi @FelipePoroca,

Yes, Power BI can consume data from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition through released OData APIs or Custom CDS Views exposed as external APIs. Direct database access is not available in the multitenant Public Cloud environment.

You may also evaluate Power BI Connector for SAP:

https://www.sap.com/products/data-cloud/partners/metrica-software-inc-power-bi-connector-for-sap.html

It lets you select SAP OData services, entities, fields, and filters, then provides an OData endpoint for Power BI.

Compatibility depends on the APIs, authentication method, and communication arrangements available in your specific Public Cloud tenant, so the setup should be verified during evaluation.

A 30-day free trial is available, and we would be happy to review your scenario during a demo.

Documentation:
https://metricasoftware.com/docs/sap/

Cheers,
Anton

entry.title: Revamped Documentation for SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Navigating complex software documentation can be challenging, especially when a product offers as many capabilities as SAP Integration Suite. After many years of growth, that's why our User Assistance team for SAP Integration Suite decided it was time for a change.

We, that is, the authors of the help documents for SAP Integration Suite, have revamped the documentation structure. You'll now find a streamlined main hub that connects you directly to individual capability guides. Simply click the capability name to jump off to the area you need – whether that's API Management, Integration Advisor, or any other capability of SAP Integration Suite.

Curious? Get exploring right away: SAP Integration Suite

GIF showing navigation from Integration Suite to Trading Partner Management guide

 

 

 

 

 

 

What This Means For You

  • Slimmer guides with flatter hierarchies - less scrolling and searching
  • Faster navigation gets you to the right information quickly
  • Focused content shows only what's relevant to your current task

... all with the same quality and surrounded by the modern look of our SAP Help Portal. 🌟

Take Action

Ready to explore? Head over to the new documentation structure and see the difference for yourself. Just remember to update your bookmarks to your favorite pages.

Let us know how you like the changes!

By the way, are you already collaboration with us? Thanks to our Open Documentation collaboration process, you can give feedback and raise issues about the documentation using GitHub. Learn more about it in under Collaborate with us on the Migration Guide for SAP Process Orchestration!

Happy exploring!

entry.title: Re: How to expose Supplier confirmation status (LFA1-CONF...
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the suggestions help you resolve your issue:

Supplier Change-Confirmation Status

Unfortunately, there is no released (C1) CDS view or OData API that directly exposes the fields CONFS, UPDAT, and UPTIM from the LFA1 table in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Since LFA1 is not released for direct access, you cannot select it in a custom CDS view or expose these fields via an extension field.

Alternative Solutions

  1. Use the "Confirm Supplier List" Fiori App (Process-Level Workaround) Rather than reading the confirmation status programmatically, leverage the standard Confirm Supplier List app to manage and monitor pending dual-control confirmations. This app surfaces all suppliers with sensitive-field changes awaiting confirmation, configured via SSCUI 102853 (Define Sensitive Fields for Dual Control – Vendors). [4] [5] While this does not expose CONFS/UPDAT/UPTIM to an external system, it covers the operational use case within the SAP UI.

  2. Custom Field and Logic: You might consider introducing a custom field and updating it using a BAdI implementation or custom logic. This approach would involve capturing the necessary data during the confirmation process and storing it in a custom field.

  3. SAP Influence Program: If the required CDS view is not available, you can submit a request through the SAP Influence Program (at https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/influence-adopt) to have the view released or to have the necessary fields exposed in a future update.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud (C4C) 2608 Pre-Release Briefing
entry.content:
Hi All,

SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud (C4C) Pre-Release Briefing presentation for the upcoming 2608 (Aug 2026) release is now available. We have some key innovations to be shared as part of SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud 2608 Release.

Platform & Integration:

Transition Tools:

  • Data Transfer Tool (DTT) | Onboarding Utilities objects data transfer from V1 to V2 - You can now onboard Utilities objects (Premise, Contract Account) in DTT to transition from V1 to V2.
  • Data Transfer Tool (DTT) | Transfer file size increase in Sales Orders and Sales Quotes - Increase in the Maximum File size limit from 4 MB to 10 MB for Sales Orders and Sales Quotes
  • Data Transfer Tool (DTT) | Introducing ‘Count Summary’ for Surveys - Count Summary is added in Survey Design and Survey Response Objects in DTT
  • Readiness Check Tool (RCT) | Hierarchical display of integration scenarios artifacts - Visual improvements in “Integration Scenarios” tab of RCT to show communication arrangements under communication system they belong to

Feeds | Integration with SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition - As an Administrator, you have an option to use SAP Jam (until 31st December 2026 due to deprecation of SAP Jam collaboration) or SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition for collaboration via Feeds. This was communicated earlier in the SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud 2511 Release Briefing. Kindly plan to move from SAP Jam and start using SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition for collaboration via Feeds based on your requirements.

SAP Service Cloud:

  • Ability to display confirmation pop-up while discarding an email draft from the Timeline tab in Tickets - Now, a confirmation pop-up is displayed when an email draft is discarded on choosing the Discard Draft action from the Timeline tab of Tickets.
  • Enabled Read access (PSM release) for BTD Reference association in the Maintenance Plan BO

SAP Agent Desktop Add-On:

  • Agent Desktop Add-On | CTI Overlay Auto-Minimize and Auto-Popup on Call Events - When an agent accepts an incoming call, the CTI Overlay automatically minimizes so the agent can focus fully on the Agent Desktop. When the customer ends the call, the CTI Overlay pops up automatically — prompting the agent to complete 'Close the Interaction' without any manual clicks.

Thanks,

SAP Sales Cloud & SAP Service Cloud Product Management Team

entry.title: Equipment Summary AI functionality in SAP Field Service Management
entry.content:
SAP Field Service Management has a quiet AI feature that shows the Equipment Summary for technicians has walked into a service job blindly at least once or to give better clarity about the work.

What it's about:

Equipment Summary uses generative AI to write a short summary (maximum 20 number of sentences per summary) of a piece of equipment based on its past activities (maximum of 20 Activities). The Content uses Activity Information, Checklist Instance, Item, Reserved Material, Time Effort, Service Call, Warehouse and User Defined Fields for all objects. The technician gets instant context, the dispatcher gets a clearer basis for assigning the right person. It shows up in Planning and Dispatching (Dispatching Board / Service Calls) and in the mobile app, with links back to the underlying details.

 

How to activate it:

It's a Premium AI feature, so it needs the GENERATIVE_AI role. The role will be assigned at Account Level.

Sections in the Summary:
It is not mandatory to keep all the sections (Flexible for customers):
1. Problem Description
2. Responsible
3. Resolution Steps
4. Root Cause
5. Parts Removed
6. Parts Installed

Advantages:

Faster first-time fixes, less time hunting through history, and smarter dispatch decisions. Very much useful for recurring issues on an equipment.

Limitations:

1. Summary uses activities with 'CLOSED' status. so open work won't appear.
2. Supports every profile language except 'Tamil'.

entry.title: Zum guten Schluss - der Knaller! : Einheit 2 - Video 6
entry.content:
An English version of this lesson is available here.

 

Videolektion "Zum guten Schluss"

In dieser Lektion hast du die Themen und Konzepte der vorherigen beiden Einheiten wiederholt, wie Schleifen, Kostüme und Grafikeffekte, Klone, Nutzerinteraktion und vieles mehr. Dabei hast du ein interaktives Spiel programmiert.

Folge den Schritten aus dem Video in Snap!. Die Aufgaben bauen teilweise auf das Projekt aus dem Video auf. 

 

 

 

Du bist dran

  • Mache dein Spiel ein wenig interessanter, indem du eine Variable hinzufügst, die den prozentualen Anteil der Ballons, die du getroffen hast, anzeigt.
    Für die Berechnung benötigst du mehrere Hilfsvariablen.

    Vergiss nicht, dass du Operatoren ineinander verschachteln kannst, um komplexere Formeln zu bauen.  

 

  • Werde selbst kreativ. Erweitere dein Spiel zum Beispiel um Objekte, die nicht getroffen werden dürfen oder baue etwas komplett anderes in Snap!

 

  • Hier findest du die Aufgaben auch zum Herunterladen:

    • als PDF: https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/rmNznrr?rc=10&doi=SAP1328497 
    • als editierbare docx Version:  https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/58W3Dgi?rc=10&doi=SAP1328496 

    Lösungsvorschläge für Einheit 2 - Lektion 6 findest du in dieser Collection.

 

 

Zusammenfassung - das hast du diese Lektion gelernt

Bausteine dieser Lektion: 

Diese Lektion ist eine Zusammenfassung der informatischen Konzepte aus den ersten beiden Einheiten. Es wurden keine neuen Blöcke verwendet. 

 

Wiederholung

Diese Lektion war eine Zusammenfassung der Inhalte, die du in den vergangenen beiden Einheiten gelernt hast.

Du hast Schleifen verwendet, um Teile deines Skripts wiederholt auszuführen. Mit Zufallswerten hast du die Position und Bewegung deines Objekts bestimmt. Grafikeffekte aus der "Aussehen" Kategorie haben die Farbe und Größe deines Ballons verändert. Außerdem hast du weitere Blöcke aus der "Aussehen" Kategorie verwendet, um zwischen den Kostümen deines Objekts zu wechseln. Das Drehen und Bewegen erfolgte mit Blöcken aus der Kategorie "Bewegung". Mit verschiedenen Startblöcken hast du die entsprechenden Skripte gestartet. Mit Blöcken, die auf Mausklicks reagieren, hast du Benutzerinteraktionen hinzugefügt. Schließlich hast du mithilfe von Klonen mehrere Agenten mit ähnlichem Verhalten zu deinem Spiel hinzugefügt.

Wenn du dein Projekt in der Snap!-Cloud speicherst, kannst du einen Link zum Projekt erstellen und ihn mit deinen Freunden, Eltern, Geschwistern oder anderen Personen teilen. So können sie dein Spiel ebenfalls auf ihrem Smartphone, Tablet oder Computer spielen.

 

Und jetzt?

Das wars mit Einheit 2 - Lektion 6: Zum guten Schluss - der Knaller!

  • Zur nächsten Lektion des Kurses geht es hier: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-young-thinkers-program-blog-posts/mit-daten-arbeiten-eine-kurze-einf%C3%BChrung-in-programmierparadigmen-einheit-3/ba-p/14448216 
  • Zur Übersichtsseite von Einheit 2 geht es hier: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-young-thinkers-program-blog-posts/einheit-2-bausteine-f%C3%BCr-syst... 
  • Zur Übersichtsseite des gesamten Kurses geht es hier: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-young-thinkers-program-blog-posts/programmieren-mit-snap-building-u... 
  • Eine Englische Version des Kurses, bei der ein Zertifikat erworben werden kann, findet sich hier: https://learning.sap.com/courses/get-coding-with-snap-building-up-to-ai 

 

Dieses Material ist veröffentlicht unter der CC 4.0 Lizenz durch das Snap! Team bei SAP

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud EWM: How to Print Only One G...
entry.content:
Hello!

The GR label mechanism in Inventory Management determines the label count from a quantity field. The label type (M7 - GR label) combined with the label form controls which labels print for which goods movement and how many print, and the delivered label forms are quantity-based variants: E1 for the entry quantity, E2 for the quantity in storage, with the equivalents for order quantity and order price quantity.

An alternative is to use the GR slip mechanism: the goods receipt slip output on the material document has an individual slip variant, which is inherently one output per material document item.

  • Clear Label Type and Label Form in the product master. Leaving these blank stops the quantity-driven label generation.
  • Use the individual-slip output type on business object Material Document, not the collective slip. One item > one output.
  • Build a custom form template in Maintain Form Templates, copy the standard GR slip template, resize it to your label stock, strip it down to material / batch / qty / delivery / barcode.
  • Fix the copy count to 1 in Output Parameter Determination. In the BRF+ decision table, NUMBER_OF_COPIES is normally mapped from V_DETERM_PARAMS-NMBROFGRORGISLIPSTOPRINTQTY (the standard rule sets the copies from that field). Replace that mapping with a constant of 1.
  • Result: one label-formatted PDF per material document item.

Make sure: material document items map 1:1 to inbound delivery items only if there's no split. Batch split, split valuation, serial number handling, or a storage-location split at GR will produce multiple material document items for one delivery item, and you'd get a label for each.

References:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/is-it-possible-to-control-number-of-labels-in-goods-receipt-print/qaq-p/12310813

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/goods-receipt-number-of-labels/qaq-p/12333397

entry.title: Asset History Sheet: Standard Key Figure for Total Movements?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am working on an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation for a customer in Portugal.

The customer provided an Excel report with the following fixed asset information by asset class:

  • Opening Acquisition Cost
  • Opening Accumulated Depreciation
  • Opening Net Book Value
  • Additions
  • Retirements
  • Depreciation for the Year
  • Total Movements
  • Closing Acquisition Cost
  • Closing Accumulated Depreciation
  • Closing Net Book Value

After reviewing the standard Asset History Sheet, I was able to map almost all of the requested values to the standard key figures:

Customer report Asset History Sheet Key Figure
Opening Acquisition CostAPC FY Start
Opening Accumulated DepreciationAccumulated Depreciation at FY Start
Opening Net Book ValueNet Book Value FY Start
AdditionsAcquisition, Current Year (+ Post-Capitalization APC, Current Year, if applicable)
RetirementsRetirements, Current Year
Depreciation for the YearDepreciation, Current Year
Closing Acquisition CostAPC at Reporting Date
Closing Accumulated DepreciationAcc. Depr. at Reporting Date
Closing Net Book ValueNet Book Value at Reporting Date

The only value I could not identify is Total Movements.

In the customer's report, this value is calculated as:

Additions (+ Post-Capitalizations, if applicable) − Retirements − Depreciation for the Year

My question is:

  • Is there any standard Key Figure in the Asset History Sheet or I_AssetHistorySheetCube that already provides this value?
  • If not, is there any standard CDS View that exposes this calculation?
  • Or is the recommended Clean Core approach in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to calculate this value externally (for example, using a Custom Analytical Query, SAP Analytics Cloud, or another reporting solution)?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: Customer Returns (BKP) – Create Credit Memo Request O...
entry.content:
Dear @Thiru2203,

No. In Public Cloud the requested sequence is not supported while retaining Refund Control = Create Credit Memo Request (P). With Refund Control P the Credit Memo Request is created when the returns order is released. Its creation is not dependent on the subsequent Goods Receipt status

More details below:

SAP Help – Customer Compensation

SAP Best Practices – Customer Returns: Approve/Reject/Rework Returns Order

The standard SAP option for postponing the Credit Memo Request until the returns process has been completed is Refund Control = Decide Later (R)

More details below:

SAP KBA 3433519 – How to avoid credit memo to be created before the end of the returns process?

Therefore if decide later cannot be used because it changes the requirement type to MSR there is no documented standard configuration that both retains Refund Control P and creates the Credit Memo Request only after Goods Receipt

The closest standard alternative is to configure a default billing block for the Credit Memo Request type GA2. The Credit Memo Request would still be created when the returns order is released but the actual Credit Memo could not be created until the Goods Receipt has been verified and the billing block has been removed manually. SAP does not provide a standard automatic dependency that removes the billing block based on the Goods Receipt status

More details below:

SAP Help – Credit Memo Requests

SAP Help – Manage Sales Document Workflows

SAP Help – Manage Credit Memo Requests – Version 2

IMPORTANT: If automatic creation of the Credit Memo Request after Goods Receipt is required while retaining Refund Control P the requirement can be submitted as an improvement request through SAP Continuous Influence

More details below:

SAP Continuous Influence for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Public Cloud: XBLNR as reference instead of accountin...
entry.content:
Confirm which tree was actually used

To see which mapping the run really applied, open the Map Format Data app and use its Trace function — the Trace button sits above the list on the Outgoing tab, or on the top right of your format's detail screen. The mapping has to be in a released version to be traced: set tracepoints on the relevant nodes, generate a payment file, then open the trace log. It shows node by node whether the engine used your copy or the standard tree. Reference: Trace Outgoing Format Mappings.

For a quicker check, download the generated file in the Manage Payment Media app and look at the output. If it still matches the standard layout, the missing Deviating Format Tree link — or the mapping still being in draft — is probably the cause.

BR
M.

entry.title: Re: Purchase Requisitions and Planner Orders for only chi...
entry.content:
Hello!

  1. Can a phantom parent suppress the planned order? 

Special procurement type 50 suppresses the procurement proposal only when the material appears as a component inside a superior BOM. The phantom logic is a pass-through that happens during the explosion of the level above: MRP explodes the parent, hits the phantom item, and immediately transfers the requirement down to the phantom's components without creating something for the phantom itself. At the top of the planning hierarchy there is no level above. The requirement lands directly on the material, there's nothing to absorb it, so MRP must create a planned order. A phantom can never be the top most planned item.

Two further points: the phantom indicator is also a PP concept tied to production BOMs (usage 1), it plays no role in sales BOM explosion. And it can be set at BOM item level as well as via SPT 50 in MRP 2, but neither placement changes the above.

 

  1.  The recommended mechanism is LUMF, not phantom:

Sales kits are supported in Public Cloud under scope item 31Q. In the LUMF variant the subitem behaves like a regular TAN sell-from-stock item for cost, price and profitability relevance, while the main item serves only as a bracket,  which is the "no order for the parent" behaviour.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/bill-of-material-variants-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-for-sales-scope-item-31q/ba-p/13560201

The BOM header gets item category TAP, which is not relevant for pricing, while the components get TAN, which is. TAP also carries no schedule lines, so no requirement is transferred for the parent and MRP has nothing to plan. Each TAN sub-item carries its own schedule line and requirement, so MRP plans each component individually, planned order if in-house, purchase requisition if externally procured, per that component's own procurement type.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/when-to-use-item-categories-tac-tam-and-taq/qaq-p/6293919

Configuration:

Header material: Sales: Sales Org 2 → item category group LUMF. Components: NORM.

BOM with usage 5 (Sales and Distribution) in Manage Bill of Materials.

Item category determination: OR + LUMF + <blank> + <blank> → TAP, and OR + NORM + <blank> + TAP → TAN.

On TAP: structure scope A (single-level) or B (multi-level), application SD01.

Header material: MRP type ND and a non-stocked material type, so nothing else tries to plan it.

With LUMF the components are what gets delivered and invoiced. Delivery note and customer invoice will list the children, not the parent. If the customer must receive and be billed for one assembled product, LUMF is the wrong variant  and ERLA (the alternative) puts the requirement back on the parent, eventually the planned order again.

  1. The alternative:

Components consumed in production belong in a production BOM (usage 1) under the material that is genuinely manufactured. If something is produced, a planned order for it isn't noise,  it is the plan, and it's what generates the dependent requirements for those components.

Components consumed at shipping: packaging, inserts, pallets, manuals  are normally not BOM-driven. Standard practice is consumption-based planning (MRP type VB reorder point, or VM) on those materials, replenished independently, optionally combined with packing instructions on the HU side.

If the shipping components must scale with each sales order rather than by reorder point, there's a clean hybrid: build the LUMF sales BOM containing only the shipping-process components plus the finished good as one of the sub-items. Then the FG sub-item gets a planned order and explodes its own production BOM for the production components; the packaging sub-items get their own PRs.

entry.title: Rethinking physical interfaces
entry.content:
If you think about where robots can be put to use straight away, executing tasks in hazardous environments comes to mind immediately: robots don't need oxygene, they can be designed to work under water or in environments which are very cold or very hot. 

One use case which appeared repeatedly on the World AI Conference 2026 and in our conversations with robotic manufacturers and integrators was the manipulation of electrical cabinets.

A key challenge for the robots is to open and close doors.

This activity is a good example of something which seems natural to us is hard to do programmatically. There are different ways of designing door locks and we master most of them intuitively in the right way. Sometimes, turning the lock requires fine and soft movements and carefully interpreting the sensory feedback of the key in the lock, sometimes doors need a dedicated kick or pull to open. We adapt the way we handle doors very flexibly, in most of the times successfull. 

I'm sure if you lean back and think of the different types of cabinets, entry doors and hatches you are able to open and close as well as the different locks you encountered, there will be a good and diverse list of solutions coming up. Now think of the problems which can occur with each of those. And now try to think about documenting the different cases.  Navigating those different cases and combining solutions you have learned across different door mechanics is a good case to explain the way large language amodels work, because it helps to be able to transfer one solution to another use case if you want to get past a closed door.

Things would be easier, if door handles and locking mechanisms were adapted to robots.  In a hybrid world, however, they would need to work both for humans and robots.

So while we need to invest time and patience into training robots to get past doors and locking mechanisms designed for humans, which are highly adaptable and can react on the many quirks of doors and locks, I'm curious to see how industrial design of doors and locks will be impacted by the rise of humanoids and robots in general!

entry.title: Re: Output/Form Determination Logic for the Exempt Purcha...
entry.content:
Hello!

 

The BAdI YY1_IVA_ORDEN_COMPRA  is invoked during output processing, which does not occur until the purchase order is approved. Before approval, no output item exists, so the custom fields holding VAT and Total are not populated and render blank.

Mechanism:

The PO has three distinct output states, not two:  

On hold:

  • Output behaviour: no output generated; messages tab not editable        
  • BAdI executed: No

Pending approval:

  • Output behaviour: print Preview only, marked "Test Copy"               
  • BAdI executed: No

Approved:

  • Output behaviour:  Full output processing
  • BAdI executed: Yes

When a PO workflow is active, output is sent and completed on approval. Where no workflow applies, output is sent on save: so POs falling outside the workflow's trigger conditions will display totals correctly. If the symptom appears intermittent across documents, this is why.

Confirmed by SAP in KBA 3051566 and KBA 3680561.

Resolution:

Move the VAT and Total derivation from output processing to document save. Populate and persist the custom fields on the PO header via a released PO custom logic BAdI at save, rather than filling them during output. Once persisted on the document, the values are available to render path including the pre-release Print Preview, and the dependency on approval status disappears.

Design requirement: recompute on any change to price, quantity, or tax code, otherwise the stored total goes blank.

Before building this, verify whether item net and tax condition amounts already exist in the form data. Download the data schema for template MM_PUR_PURCHASE_ORDER in Maintain Form Templates. If the tax values are present, do the arithmetic in the form template instead, no custom fields, no BAdI, and it renders in preview automatically. Template editing requires Adobe LiveCycle Designer 10.4 or higher.

entry.title: First Nations Tax Return Now Available in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content:
What Is First Nations Tax?

First Nations Tax (FNT) is a consumption tax imposed by certain First Nations governments in Canada on specified goods sold on or delivered to their reserve lands. It is administered by the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) within the same framework as Goods and Services Tax / Harmonized Sales Tax (GST/HST). Where FNT applies, it replaces the federal GST or the federal portion of HST on those goods.

FNT reporting follows the same filing periods and deadlines as GST/HST. However, the FNT Schedule (Form GST499-1) is a separate submission from the standard GST/HST return — businesses must file both forms for the corresponding reporting period to complete their statutory obligations.

Previously, companies selling listed products on First Nations reserves had no dedicated reporting task for FNT in SAP — they could not report FNT separately from GST/HST, and there was no system support for generating the data required to complete Form GST499-1. This is exactly the gap the new report addresses.

 

What the Report Delivers

The Canada FNT Return (report category CA_GST_FNT_REP) runs within the Run Statutory Reports - Canada GL Reporting (F1515_CA) app and enables companies to:

  • Dedicated FNT reporting task: Handle FNT reporting through a standalone process, ensuring no disruption to existing GST/HST reporting activities.
  • Preview Chart 1 data — aggregated FNT tax base and tax amounts required for Chart 1 of Form GST499-1
  • Preview Chart 2 data — breakdown of FNT amounts by First Nations territory/band council, required for Chart 2 of Form GST499-1
  • Download the report output in PDF format for review and mailing to the CRA
  • Manage tax items — manually include or exclude individual tax items for a reporting period
  • Analyze report data in an analytical view, with export to spreadsheet
  • Handle corrections — generate correction, additional correction, or clarification submissions for already-filed periods
  • Track and update submission status after filing with authorities 

Notes:

  • Electronic submission of Form GST499-1 is not available, as the CRA does not currently support this option — this is not an SAP limitation. Once completed, the form must be mailed to the CRA following the submission instructions provided on the CRA website.
  • One Time Customer and One Time Vendor are not supported in this release.

 

Availability

Deployment

Status

SAP S/4HANA 2025

Available — see SAP Notes below

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025

Available — see SAP Notes below

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Planned for release 2608

 

Required SAP Notes for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition

To activate the First Nations Tax Return functionality in SAP S/4HANA 2025 (and the corresponding SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition releases), you must implement two SAP Notes in the correct sequence. Both notes were released on July 17, 2026.

Implementation Order

Step 1 — Implement the main delivery note

SAP Note 3757244 — DRC Canada: First Nations Tax – GST499-1 First Nations Tax (FNT) Schedule report

This is the core note. It delivers:

  • The new CA_GST_FNT_REP report category and report definition
  • A dedicated FNT reporting task, separate from GST/HST
  • Chart 1 and Chart 2 preview data for Form GST499-1
  • PDF download of the report output
  • The new Customizing activity: Map Location to Transportation Zone for First Nation Tax

Note: Manual post-implementation steps are required. After implementing this note, download the attachment "Post-implementation steps - SAP Note 3757244.docx" directly from the note in SAP for Me and follow the steps described. This is mandatory for the solution to work correctly.

Step 2 — Implement the navigation filter update

SAP Note 3780199 — DRC Canada: First Nations Tax – Update to Run Statutory Reports filter

This note ensures that the CA_GST_FNT_REP report category appears correctly in the navigation filter of the Run Statutory Reports tile for Canada. Without this note, the report category may not be visible in the tile filter even after implementing Note 3757244.

Support Package Alternative

If you prefer to apply a Support Package rather than manual correction instructions, both notes are included in SAPK-10902INS4CORE (S4CORE) and SAPK-10902INUIS4H (UIS4H). SAP recommends applying the Support Package where possible; use the SAP Note Assistant for manual implementation only if you need the fix before the Support Package can be applied.

 

How to Get Started: Configuration

Once the SAP Notes are implemented, setting up the FNT Return report involves three Customizing steps.

Step 1 — Activate Document and Reporting Compliance

Ensure that Document and Reporting Compliance has been activated for Canada in your system. This is a one-time Customizing activity that unlocks all statutory reporting capabilities for the country.

Step 2 — Set Up the FNT Return Report

Define reporting preferences using the Setting Up Your Statutory Reporting Customizing activity. Key configuration includes:

  • Adding report category CA_GST_FNT_REP in reporting entity CA_GST_ENT

  • Setting the report periodicity aligned with your GST/HST filing cadence
  • Assigning your company code(s) to the reporting entity
  • Entering your CRA GST Registration ID (parameter CA_GST_REGISTRATION)
  • Setting the FNT specific tax box structure type (parameter TAXBOX_STR_TYPE)

Step 3 — Make Additional Settings

Complete the following Customizing activities under Document and Reporting Compliance > Country/Region-Specific Settings > Canada > Statutory Reporting > GST/FNT:

  • Maintain tax jurisdiction codes (if not already configured) — necessary to correctly identify FNT-applicable transactions. If an external tax engine is in use, ensure its jurisdiction codes are maintained here as well.
  • Maintain tax box structures — define how tax data maps into the form boxes of Form GST499-1.
  • Map delivery locations to First Nations territories — required to populate Chart 2. Use the new Map Location to Transportation Zone for First Nation Tax customizing activity.

Note:

  • The FNT report reads the transportation zone configured on each business partner. This transportation zone must be mapped in the new Map Location to Transportation Zone for First Nation Tax customizing activity. If Chart 2 shows no data, the most likely cause is a missing or mismatched transportation zone mapping.

 

Generating the Report

Once configuration is complete, end users generate the FNT Return from the Run Statutory Reports - Canada GL Reporting (F1515_CA) app:

  1. Open the app from the SAP Fiori launchpad
  2. Search for report definition CA_GST_FNT_REP (Canada FNT Return)
  3. Select the reporting task and manage tax items if needed
  4. Choose Canada FNT Reporting, start a New Run, and specify your parameters
  5. Review the Chart 1 and Chart 2 preview data
  6. Download the PDF output and submit Form GST499-1 to the CRA
  7. Update the submission status in the system after filing

 

Where to Find the Documentation

  • First Nations Tax Return — SAP Help Portal
  • SAP Note 3757244 — DRC Canada: First Nations Tax – GST499-1 FNT Schedule report (SAP for Me login required)
  • SAP Note 3780199 — DRC Canada: First Nations Tax – Update to Run Statutory Reports filter (SAP for Me login required)

 

What's Coming in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

For customers on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the First Nations Tax Return is on the roadmap and planned for release 2608. Stay tuned for updates as we get closer to that release.

 

Your Feedback Matters

This report was developed to address a real compliance need for Canadian businesses operating in First Nations territories. If you have questions, configuration feedback, or suggestions, please leave a comment below or reach out through the SAP Community.

entry.title: Einheit 2 - Bausteine für Systeme: Programmieren mit Snap! - Building up to AI
entry.content:
You can find an English version of Unit 2 - Building Systems here. 

 

Über Einheit 2 - Bausteine für Systeme

Die zweite Einheit des Kurses "Programmieren mit Snap! - Building up to AI" haben wir Bausteine für Systeme genannt. In dieser Einheit wollen wir uns mit Konzepten beschäftigen, die unsere Programme skalieren lassen, also dafür sorgen, dass wir aus wenig Code komplexe Systeme bauen können. 

Einheit 2 besteht aus 6 Lektionen, in denen unter anderem die folgenden informatischen Konzepte thematisiert werden: 

  • benutzerdefinierte Funktionen mit Rückgabewerten
  • zusammengesetzte Datentypen (Listen)
  • Grundlagen der Objektorientierung
  • Rekursion

Jede Lektion besteht aus einem Video, Übungen, die auf das Projekt aus dem Video aufbauen, sowie eine schriftliche Zusammenfassung der erlernten Blöcke und Konzepte. 

 

 

Zu den Materialien:

Lektion 1: Mit dem Chaos aufräumen

In dieser Lektion lernst du eine der grundlegenden Ideen der Objektorientierung kennen, dass Objekte "first-class" sind, das heißt in Variablen gespeichert oder als Ein- und Ausgabewert von Funktionen verwendet werden können. Dabei lässt du aus Chaos Ordnung entstehen.

https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-young-thinkers-program-blog-posts/mit-dem-chaos-aufr%C3%A4umen-einheit-2-lektion-1/ba-p/14389495 

 

Lektion 2: Gerüchteküche - erste eigene Funktionen

In Lektion 2 lernst du den Datentyp Liste kennen und schreibst deine ersten eigenen Blöcke mit Rückgabewert, sogenannte Funktionen. Daraus erzeugst du zufällig generierte Sätze und verbreitest mit Alonzo wilde Gerüchte.

https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-young-thinkers-program-blog-posts/ger%C3%BCchtek%C3%BCche-erste-eigene-funktionen-einheit-2-lektion-2/ba-p/14433306  

 

Lektion 3: Klonen - aus eins mach viele

In dieser Lektion lernst du, wie du Objekte klonen kannst, das heißt Schwärme von Objekten erzeugen, die sich ähnlich verhalten. Daraus programmierst du eine sich bewegende Blume. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-young-thinkers-program-blog-posts/klonen-aus-eins-mach-viele-einheit-2-lektion-3/ba-p/14442210 

 

Lektion 4: Rekursion - einen Baum wachsen lassen

In Lektion 4 erfährst du, wie du auch ohne Schleifen Programmteile wiederholen kannst. Nämlich indem du benutzerdefinierte Blöcke in ihren eigenen Definitionen aufrufst - ein Konzept, das Rekursion genannt wird. Damit kannst du wunderschöne Bäume wachsen lassen. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-young-thinkers-program-blog-posts/rekursion-einen-baum-wachsen-lassen-einheit-2-lektion-4/ba-p/14442831 

 

Lektion 5: Feuerwerk - Klone deine Rekursion

In dieser Lektion kombinierst du die beiden Konzepte der vorherigen Projekte - Klone und Rekursion, um im wahrsten Sinne des Wortes ein Feuerwerk zur Explosion zu bringen. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-young-thinkers-program-blog-posts/feuerwerk-klone-deine-rekursion-einheit-2-lektion-5/ba-p/14445970 

 

Lektion 6: Zum guten Schluss - der Knaller!

In dieser Lektion werden einige Konzepte noch einmal zusammengefasst. Mit Schleifen, Grafikeffekten, Kostümwechseln, Klonen und Benutzerinteraktion baust du ein Videospiel, bei dem du Ballons abwirfst. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-young-thinkers-program-blog-posts/zum-guten-schluss-der-knaller-einheit-2-video-6/ba-p/14447067 

 

Wir wünschen Dir viel Spaß mit Einheit 2 - Bausteine für Systeme.
Zur nächsten Einheit 3 - Bausteine für Künstliche Intelligenz geht es hier: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-young-thinkers-program-blog-posts/einheit-3-bausteine-f%C3%BCr-k%C3%BCnstliche-intelligenz-programmieren-mit-snap/ba-p/14448251

entry.title: Re: Problem with discarding entity using CAP as a proxy o...
entry.content:
Thanks for sharing the comparison. This makes the issue much clearer.

Since the draft/discard flow works when Fiori Elements consumes the RAP service directly, I would focus on the CAP proxy.

One thing stands out. FE requests:

QuotationHeader(...,IsActiveEntity=false)/SiblingEntity

But the request that finally reaches RAP seems to be:

QuotationHeader(...,IsActiveEntity=false)

So it looks like the SiblingEntity navigation is getting lost while CAP forwards the request.

At this point the draft has already been discarded, so the request with IsActiveEntity=false returning a 404 makes sense.

I would check req.query inside your CAP READ handler for the SiblingEntity call and see whether the navigation is still present when it reaches:

SolutionQuotationSrv.run(req.query)

I wouldn't manually switch IsActiveEntity=false to true yet, as this could affect other draft requests. First I would try to find out why the SiblingEntity navigation disappears during forwarding.

Since the same flow works with FE directly against RAP, the RAP draft handling itself seems fine. The issue looks more likely to be in the CAP proxy handling of the draft navigation.

Hope this helps narrow it down.

entry.title: Consume one material on different storage location depending on the backflush process
entry.content:
The SAP version is S/4hana 2023.

The transaction is running on Fiori.

We use the IM and EWM

The material is consumed from Storage Location 2001 when using Inventory Management (IM), and from Storage Location 1001 when using EWM.

We are using Control Cycles together with Production Supply Areas to determine the staging process.

I have configured several objects where the storage location can be defined, including:

  • Material Master (MM02)
  • Bill of Material (CS02)
  • Production Supply Area
  • Control Cycles (PKMC, PK05)

I understand that it is not possible to determine the storage location based directly on the Backflush indicator. However, I expected to be able to control it through the BOM or the Work Center (Supply area).

The issue is that, when the production order is created, the system always determines Storage Location 1001 for the consumption of this material, even though I expect it to use Storage Location 2001 in this scenario.

Could you please advise which configuration or determination logic is overriding the storage location during production order creation?

Thank you.

entry.title: how to move serialized stock to Fixed Asset when outbound delivery PGI is fixed to movement type 601
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am working on **SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition**and would appreciate input from anyone who has implemented an equipment rental model.

**Business scenario**

We rent serialized POS hardware terminals to our customers. Ownership always stays with us. The lifecycle is:

1. Terminals are received into inventory and held as stock in our warehouse
2. When a terminal is rented, it is shipped to the customer and should be treated as a Fixed Asset — it must depreciate while on rental
3. The monthly rental fee is billed through SAP Subscription Billing
4. At the end of the rental contract the terminal is physically returned to our warehouse and becomes available stock again

**The blocker**

To move the terminal from inventory to Fixed Asset I would normally use movement type 241 (GI to Fixed Asset). However, the physical shipment to the customer goes through an outbound delivery, and Post Goods Issue from an outbound delivery uses a fixed movement type (601). In Public Edition there is no way to change this — movement types are not exposed in Central Business Configuration, and there is no equivalent to OMJJ or to changing the movement type on the schedule line category.

So the reclassification to Fixed Asset cannot be tied to the delivery.

If I try to post the reclassification separately, I hit a sequencing problem either way:

- If I post 241 **before** the delivery, the stock is consumed and there is nothing left to create the outbound delivery against
- If I deliver **first**, 601 has already issued the stock

1. Is there a supported way in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to reclassify stock to a Fixed Asset when the physical movement must go through an outbound delivery?
2. Is there a standard scope item or SAP-recommended pattern for rental fleet equipment where ownership is retained and units cycle between stock and Fixed Assets?

Any experience or pointers to documentation would be very helpful.

Thanks,
Faisal

entry.title: Re: Does SAP SIGNAVIO Journey Modeler allow the creation...
entry.content:
Hi @TiagoCassiano76 

My answer as far as I know with be no, as SAP Signavio Journey Modeler currently does not support multilingual content management in the same way as Process Modeler. Multi-language functionality is available for process diagrams in Process Modeler, but there is no equivalent language configuration or translation feature documented for Journey Modeler. If multiple languages are required, the usual workaround is to maintain separate journey models for each language.

entry.title: Re: Error in OData call made with self sign certificate
entry.content: Hi Siddharth, A 404 in this case looks more like an OData service/URL issue than a problem with the X.509 certificate itself. I would first try the metadata URL directly: https://<host>:<port>/sap/opu/odata/sap/<SERVICE_NAME>/$metadata If this also returns 404, check whether the service is actually registered and active in the SAP system. Depending on your Gateway setup, check the service in /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE and verify the corresponding ICF node in SICF. Also make sure that the URL uses the correct technical service name and, if required, the correct SAP client (sap-client). Once $metadata works independently, I would then move on to the certificate authentication and Python side. If possible, could you share the URL structure you're calling (without hostname/sensitive information) and whether $metadata works from a browser or Postman?

entry.title: Re: T811s Table currency field (FACURR) not changing when...
entry.content:
Hi Vittal,

When the sender rule is "Posted Amounts" and you switch the currency to object
currency (GBP), the cycle reads the GBP amount that was stored at the original
posting rate. But when it posts to the receiver, SAP converts back to USD at
today's rate. Since the rates are different, the sender cost center will not
zero out — you will always have a small USD amount left over that is hard to
explain.

On top of that, if your cycle has senders and receivers in different currencies
(for example some in GBP and some in EUR), SAP will ignore the object currency
setting and fall back to CO area currency anyway, so the change has no effect
for those segments.

The safe approach is to keep the sender currency as CO area currency (USD) for
"Posted Amounts" cycles. If you need to allocate in local currency, use "Fixed
Amounts" instead and enter the GBP value manually.

Reference: SAP Note 2914540 — Free currencies in CO allocations

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: descriptions of internal orders are not displayed in...
entry.content:
Hello Sir,

SAP Note 3466596 ("KOH3 - Internal order short text missing") covers your exact symptom.

Why it happens: KOH3 uses display mode, which reads the order description table using binary search. However, the table is not built in sorted order, so binary search silently skips entries and descriptions appear blank. KOH2 uses sequential search in change mode, which always finds everything — that is why descriptions show up there but not in KOH3.

Fix: Apply SAP Note 3276864 via SNOTE. It corrects the table-building logic so entries are inserted in sorted order, and binary search then works correctly in display mode. Valid for S4CORE 102–109 (check the SP level in the note for your release).

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: Best Practice Activation Error/SMB99/CRTX_CRHD_LANG_O...
entry.content:
Hello Sir,

The eCATT script /SMB99/CRTX_CRHD_LANG_O001 (Building Block 7YA — Work Center) is a "ReplaceText" step: it tries to update the description texts of sample Work Centers that come with the US demo content. The variables US_VAR001, US_VAR002, etc. are the IDs of those sample Work Centers. The error "Entry does not exist in" means the script looked for those sample Work Centers in your system but could not find them.

A similar pattern is documented in SAP Note 3630799 ("Getting error 'Entry SM_PROD01 does not exist in MARA' in Best Practice Activation without Demo Data") for scope item US_41Z_OP: when activating without demo/sample data, eCATT demo data activities produce this type of error because the sample objects they target were never created. The resolution there is to skip the step.

However, no SAP Note has been confirmed for this specific eCATT (CRTX_CRHD_LANG_O001) or scope item US_7VG_OP / BB 7YA. We would recommend opening an OSS incident under component SV-CLD-PC to get a definitive answer on whether this step can be safely skipped in your scenario.

entry.title: Re: Email Notification for Credit-Blocked Sales Order Wit...
entry.content:
Hey Yahyaa! Great question. If you’re looking to bypass the standard approval dependency (like the 1QM scope item) and just need a way to notify the credit director, here are a few standard alternatives you can explore:

1. Custom Situation Handling

Instead of trying to reuse the standard FIN_DCD_APPROVAL_2 template, you can build a custom Situation Type based on the UKM_DCD (Documented Credit Decision) object. Just set your trigger condition for when a DCD is created with status = 10 (New). From there, you can configure the action to shoot an informational email over to the credit director using team assignments. It completely avoids the approval workflow and simply reacts to the creation of the block.

2. Business Events & Communication Arrangements

Another clean extensibility option is leveraging standard business events. When a sales order hits a credit block, the system automatically raises an event (e.g., CreditCheckBlocked). You can subscribe to this via SAP Event Mesh or SAP Integration Suite and route it straight to an email service like Outlook, SMTP, or SAP Build Process Automation.

3. Output Management (BRF+)

Technically, you could also set up an output determination rule for the sales order credit block scenario. This tool is usually reserved for customer-facing documents, so it’s a bit less elegant for this specific use case, but you can definitely use it to generate a custom "internal notification" output type that emails the director. Hope this points you in the right direction, man! Let me know which route you end up taking or if you need to bounce any more ideas around.

entry.title: Re: Contingent worker in Compensation Module SSF
entry.content:
@Raluca_Barbacariu 

You need to troubleshoot, it shouldn't too hard, it's all about being methodical.

Duplicate the template, remove any eligibility rule tied to the duplicated template, then in Plan Setup > Settings switch to everyone is eligible. This catches everyone who is a valid EC user in the system and who has a job info and comp info record as of the template effective date from (Plan Setup> EC Settings). Launch a worksheet and check that she is being added.

If she is still not added then check her job info and comp info records and make sure the date of the very first record is on or before your template's effective date.

Best,

Xavier

The SAP Academic Meetup Chile 2026 is bringing together educators, researchers, and academic leaders for a day of learning, collaboration, and innovation.

Whether you're already part of the SAP University Alliances community or interested in discovering how SAP technologies can enhance teaching and learning, this event is the perfect opportunity to connect with peers, exchange ideas, and explore the latest developments in digital transformation, sustainability, and artificial intelligence.

Throughout the event, participants will gain insights into SAP University Alliances initiatives, hear from industry and academic experts, and discover new opportunities to collaborate with institutions across Chile. The meetup will also provide a valuable platform for networking, sharing best practices, and discussing how academia and industry can work together to prepare students for the future workforce.

By participating, you'll become part of a growing academic community committed to equipping the next generation with the skills needed to thrive in an increasingly digital world.

Location: SAP Chile, Santiago
Registration: Free of charge (registration required)

entry.title: Re: SF Compensation Budget Rule Configuration
entry.content:
Hi @Ajnos18 

As per David's comment we don't have a feature to do this per se.

However if you use a budget of type "PercentOfCustomField" with a custom column to calculate individual enveloppe for each employee, and another one to calculate the spend, then all you need to do is to make a third custom column with a custom validation with a HARD stop that says "If spent column value isn't equal  (or also greater if you let them overspend) to envelopppe column value, then trigger hard stop".

This way they can't even SAVE the worksheet which means they can't route it either. If you need to have the hard stop also in Executive Review you can check the 

Two blogs to help you with the design:

An example of a custom validation with a different business case: https://blogs.sap.com/2020/08/10/custom-validation-to-prevent-promotion-dollar-entries-without-actual-promotion/

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/executive-review-alerts-for-budget-overspend/ba-p/13801402 

Best,

Xavier

entry.title: Train-the-Trainers | SAP Sustainability Footprint Management
entry.content:
Enhance Your Sustainability Expertise with the SAP Sustainability Footprint Management Train-the-Trainers

Sustainability is becoming a strategic priority for organizations worldwide, and universities play a key role in preparing future professionals to address this challenge. To support educators in bringing these topics into the classroom, SAP University Alliances is offering an exclusive Train-the-Trainers: SAP Sustainability Footprint Management program, in Portuguese.

This online training is designed for faculty members from institutions participating in the SAP University Alliances program who want to expand their knowledge of sustainability and learn how to incorporate SAP Sustainability Footprint Management (SFM) into their teaching. Participants will gain both conceptual and practical insights into one of SAP's leading sustainability solutions, which enables organizations to calculate and manage corporate and product carbon footprints using business data.

The program combines an introductory webinar with two interactive hands-on sessions, providing participants with the knowledge and confidence to apply sustainability concepts in an academic environment. Throughout the training, attendees will explore key capabilities of SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, discover classroom use cases, and exchange experiences with fellow educators from across Latin America and the Caribbean.

Whether you are looking to enrich your sustainability curriculum, stay up to date with SAP's latest innovations, or strengthen your students' digital and sustainability skills, this Train-the-Trainers program offers a valuable opportunity to grow professionally while becoming part of a collaborative academic community.

Program Highlights

  • Sessions: Exclusive Hands-on Training for Faculty about SAP Sustainability Footprint Management 
  • Format: Live online sessions (Webinar)
  • Audience: Faculty members from SAP University Alliances institutions
  • Participation: Free of charge (registration required)

Join us and discover how SAP Sustainability Footprint Management can help bring sustainability, innovation, and real-world business scenarios into your classroom.

Register today and learn more on the event page:

https://events.sap.com/lac-sapsustainabilityfootprintmanagementpt2026/en_us/home.html

entry.title: Train-the-Trainers | SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Empower Your Teaching with SAP S/4HANA Hands-on Training

As organizations continue to accelerate their digital transformation, professionals with expertise in SAP S/4HANA are more in demand than ever. To help educators prepare students for this evolving business landscape, SAP University Alliances is offering an exclusive SAP S/4HANA Hands-on Program designed specifically for faculty members.

This live online training provides participants with practical experience using SAP S/4HANA and the Global Bike 4.3 model company, enabling them to explore end-to-end business processes in a modern enterprise environment. Through guided exercises and real-world scenarios, educators will strengthen their understanding of SAP's next-generation ERP and learn how to effectively integrate these concepts into their courses.

Over the course of the program, attendees will work through core business functions, gaining hands-on experience with SAP Fiori applications and key enterprise processes such as Financial Accounting, Sales, Procurement, Production Planning, and Materials Management. The training also introduces best practices for teaching SAP S/4HANA in higher education, helping faculty deliver engaging, industry-relevant learning experiences.

Beyond technical knowledge, the program offers an excellent opportunity to connect with fellow educators from across Latin America and the Caribbean, exchange teaching experiences, and become part of a collaborative academic community committed to developing the next generation of SAP professionals.

Whether you're looking to update your curriculum, expand your SAP expertise, or provide students with valuable hands-on ERP experience, this program will equip you with the knowledge and confidence to bring SAP S/4HANA into the classroom.

Program Highlights

  • Live, instructor-led online sessions
  • Hands-on exercises using SAP S/4HANA and Global Bike 4.3
  • Real-world end-to-end business process scenarios
  • Best practices for teaching SAP in higher education
  • Networking opportunities with faculty across Latin America and the Caribbean
  • Free participation for eligible SAP University Alliances faculty (registration required)

Join us and discover how SAP S/4HANA can help transform the way you teach enterprise systems while preparing students with the digital skills employers are looking for.

Register today and learn more on the event page:

https://events.sap.com/trainingsaps4hana2026/en_us/home.html

entry.title: Re: Multiple/concurrent employment in Compensation Module
entry.content:
Hi @Raluca_Barbacariu 

This is a FAQ that has already been answered in previous posts here. 

We don't have a way to merge records and pick and choose which worksheet they will show on without manual intervention.

As a consequence none of your 3 questions is possible unfortunately (except maybe 3 with eligibility rules) unless you build a lot of integrations to bring data over from one profile to another or from both profiles to an MDF object which is honestly a white elephant we don't recommend building.

For compensation, the leading practice is : only one record out of both is retrieved. You need to have a field on EC job info (leading practice design) that helps you build the eligibility rule accordingly (for example exclude if value in Home/Host field = Host). Then whoever is the manager of the record being retrieved picks up their phone and calls the other one from the other record that got excluded to determine the increase that will be given.

For variable pay it's a little different, here the leading practice is for the bonus to be paid by each legal entity the employee works in. If you need one of the entities to take up the entire payout and as a consequence publish back to EC on one of the two profiles and also show the full payout on the same statement them then you will have no choice but to show your customer how they can export the record(s) retrieved from the EC profile they DON'T WANT to publish back to, modify it and import it into the userID of the record they WANT to publish back to. Here you should check out this blog I made not long ago: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/understanding-the-importance-of-legacy-bonus-plan-eligibility-rules-in/ba-p/14230793

Best,

Xavier

entry.title: Manage Service Keys for a Decentralized Identity Verification Tenant
entry.content:
When you provision a DIV wallet, the Provisioning Service creates an initial service key with full administrative access. This key is suitable for setup and administration but handing it to every application or integration partner exposes more than they need. The DIV Provisioning Service lets you create additional service keys with a precisely scoped set of authorities and, optionally, restricted to specific applications within the wallet.

This post shows how to create a restricted service key, what scopes are available, and walks through a concrete example: a key for a Catena-X dataspace participant that can exchange credentials but cannot administer the wallet.

Two Ways to Create a Service Key

You can create an additional service key either through the DIV Provisioning Service UI or directly via the API. Both approaches produce the same result, choose based on whether you're doing a one-off setup or automating key provisioning.

Via the UI

Open the DIV Provisioning Service admin UI, navigate to your wallet, and select "Create Service Key." The form lets you enter a name, select authorities from a list, and optionally specify which applications the key should access.

Via the API

For automated or programmatic key creation, use the DIV Provisioning Service API. The operation follows the same async pattern as wallet provisioning: POST the operation, then poll for completion.

API Call: Create a Restricted Service Key

The endpoint is POST /api/v1.0.0/operations with entity: customer-wallet-key and action: create. The payload specifies which wallet to create the key for, which authorities to grant, and optionally which applications the key can access.

# Step 1: Get an OAuth token
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST "$UAA_URL/oauth/token"   -u "$CLIENT_ID:$CLIENT_SECRET"   -d "grant_type=client_credentials" | jq -r '.access_token')

# Step 2: Create the service key
RESPONSE=$(curl -si -X POST "$PROV_URL/api/v1.0.0/operations"   -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"   -H "Content-Type: application/json"   -d '{
    "entity": "customer-wallet-key",
    "action": "create",
    "payload": {
      "customerWalletId": "<your-wallet-id>",
      "divWalletServiceName": "main",
      "divWalletServiceParameters": {
        "authorities": [
          "ResolveDID", "VerifiablePresentation",
          "ReadApplication", "ReadCompanyIdentity",
          "ReadVerifiableCredential", "DcpOperations",
          "DcpHolderOperations", "IatpOperations"
        ],
        "accessibleApplications": ["catena-x"]
      }
    }
  }')

OP_ID=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | grep -i '^location:' | sed 's|.*/||' | tr -d '
')
curl -s "$PROV_URL/api/v1.0.0/operations/$OP_ID" -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq '.status,.data.serviceKey'

Available Authorities

The following authorities can be assigned when creating a service key. They fall into two categories: admin scopes for wallet configuration, and application scopes for credential operations.

Admin Scopes

Authority What it allows

ModifyApplicationCreate/update/delete applications
ModifyCompanyIdentityUpdate company identity configuration
ModifyIssuerConfigurationUpdate issuer configuration
ModifySchemaCreate/update/delete credential schemas
ModifyWebhookConfigurationManage webhook configuration
ManageRevocationListManage credential revocation lists
ReadNetworksRead network/trust registry configuration
UpdateNetworksUpdate network/trust registry configuration

Application Scopes

Authority What it allows

ReadApplicationRead application data
ReadCompanyIdentityRead company identity / DID documents
ReadRevocationListRead credential revocation lists
ReadSchemaRead credential schemas
ReadTrustedIssuerRead trusted issuers
ModifyTrustedIssuerAdd/update/delete trusted issuers
ReadVerifiableCredentialRead verifiable credentials
ModifyVerifiableCredentialIssue/revoke verifiable credentials
ResolveDIDResolve DIDs
VerifiablePresentationCreate/verify verifiable presentations
DcpOperationsGeneral DCP operations
DcpHolderOperationsDCP holder-side operations
DcpIssuerOperationsDCP issuer-side operations
IatpOperationsIATP operations
IssuerOperationsOID4VCI issuer operations
HolderOperationsOID4VCI holder operations

Example: Catena-X Dataspace Participant Key

A Catena-X participant needs to resolve DIDs, present and verify credentials, and perform DCP-based data exchange. It does not need to configure the wallet, manage schemas, or issue new credentials. The key is also scoped to the catena-x application, preventing accidental access to other applications in the same wallet.

"divWalletServiceParameters": {
  "authorities": [
    "ResolveDID", "VerifiablePresentation",
    "ReadApplication", "ReadCompanyIdentity",
    "ReadVerifiableCredential", "DcpOperations",
    "DcpHolderOperations", "IatpOperations"
  ],
  "accessibleApplications": ["catena-x"]
}

This key can participate in dataspace credential exchanges but cannot modify wallet configuration, create schemas, manage revocation lists, or access any application other than catena-x.

References

  • API documentation (APIHub)
  • SAP Help Portal

entry.title: How to run different versions of the ABAP development tools for Visual Studio Code in parallel?
entry.content:
Introduction

Having now released a second version of the ABAP development tools for Visual Studio Code the question may arise how to run different versions of this plugin in parallel in order to test the latest version or to check differences in the behavior of the new and an older version.

At least SAP internally this is a question that is of interest for me since I want to run the public available version and the next upcoming version before this is going to be released officially 😉.

For the ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse you have to install a second Eclipse so that you can test a second version there.

How to section

Co-Pilot will provide various options to this question from which I chose the IMHO most easiest one, namely to create different profiles and install a different extension version in each profile.

You can either click on the gear icon and select Profiles.

Or you can open Command Palette (Ctrl+Shift+P) and then enter Profiles and select Profiles: New Profile.

This will open the following dialogue where you can select the default profile from the drop down box.

This let you swith to the newly created profile.

Using the drop down arrow of the "uninstall" button you are offered to install a specific version of the selected extension in the copied profile.

Here you are able to select an older version

As a result you can activate this older version by restarting the extension.

Please note that the Autoupdate option has been deactivated conveniently.
